lesson.menu_title: Planning Ahead — Talking About Future HR Projects and Strategic Decisions
lesson.menu_desc: Talking about decided plans and evidence-based predictions with 'going to' -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 6 -- Planning Ahead -- Future Plans with Going To
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 6 -- Planning Ahead

listenings.text: Hi Murielle, it's Liam. Let me walk you through our plans for next quarter, so we are aligned. First, we are going to expand the Australian team by ten people. That is our main goal for the quarter, and the budget is already approved. We are also going to set up a new onboarding program, because the current one is too slow. Our target is simple: we want every new hire ready in two weeks, not four. On the strategy side, we are not going to hire externally for the senior roles; instead, we are going to promote from inside. I am going to send you the full roadmap on Friday, so please look at the priorities before our call. I really think this plan is going to work. Talk soon.

Good morning, Murielle. This is Diana from the London office, with a quick forecast before the board meeting. Look at the numbers from this quarter: applications are up thirty percent, and our costs are down. Based on this evidence, I think next year is going to be a strong year for hiring. We are going to reach our headcount target early, and we are probably going to expand into two new markets. However, I want to be honest: if we do not update the system soon, the team is going to struggle with the extra work. So my recommendation is clear. We are going to deliver the growth plan, but we are also going to invest in better tools first. I am sending you the full strategy now. Let's discuss the priorities tomorrow. Thank you.
